fault_hypothesis: On line 68, the average is computed by dividing `total` by `window` instead of `len(tail)`. When the series has fewer elements than the window, this produces an incorrect (too-small) average.

constraints.intent: After computing avg, it must equal total divided by the actual number of samples in tail, not by window.

spec.expr: len(tail__1) == window or abs(avg__1 - total__1 / len(tail__1)) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: avg__1 must be consistent with dividing by the actual tail length when tail is shorter than window.
Before avg__1 is returned, it must equal total divided by the actual number of elements in tail.
The tail can have at most window elements; if fewer, dividing by window gives a wrong average.
